question_groups.instruction:
Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text.
Choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information.
Choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

items.prompt_html: All of the baobab species found on Madagascar are unique to the island.
Adanson and Grandidier were the first foreign botanists to visit Madagascar.
The baobab can be found in most areas of Madagascar.
The landscape around the Avenue des Baobabs is the result of agricultural development.
The baobab provides useful products for the local people.
There is general agreement about the reasons for baobabs falling down.

options.label: TRUE
options.text: if the statement agrees with the information

options.label: FALSE
options.text: if the statement contradicts the information

options.label: NOT GIVEN
options.text: if there is no information on this

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Complete the notes below.

Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Baobabs under threat

Reasons why Madagascar's baobabs are declining in number:

• Morondava area

- trees may be falling down because of <blank data-qid="q7"/> from nearby fields

- lone trees are at risk from <blank data-qid="q8"/>

• Forest areas

- land clearance: mainly by burning carried out to create space for <blank data-qid="q9"/> activity

- practice of <blank data-qid="q10"/> makes seed germination difficult

- disappearance of certain <blank data-qid="q11"/> which dispersed baobab seeds

Jim Bond’s efforts to record information about the baobab:

- making <blank data-qid="q12"/> of areas of relatively undamaged baobab forest

- producing a <blank data-qid="q13"/> showing importance of baobab in local culture

question_groups.instruction:
Reading Passage 2 has seven paragraphs.
Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.

options.label: iii
options.text: More gold than silver in Greece

options.label: iv
options.text: Calculating what a coin was worth

options.label: v
options.text: A simpler, more efficient way of trading

options.label: vi
options.text: Putting a face to famous historical names

options.label: A
options.text: There is no record of the names of the coin makers.

options.label: B
options.text: Coins were not popular as payment for traded objects.

options.label: C
options.text: Coins with the same design often looked quite different.

options.label: D
options.text: Greek coins were more beautiful in design than Roman coins.

options.label: E
options.text: At times, too many coins were produced in Greece.

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the summary below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Complete the summary below.

Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Greek traders and coins

The ancient Greek <blank data-qid="q23"/> was made up of many city-states which were spread over a large area. Many Greeks were seafarers who travelled the seas trading. Before coins, they had to do this through <blank data-qid="q24"/> . This meant that, whenever they set out, they had to carry a lot of goods, which could get damaged on the voyages. The Greeks liked coins; they were made of metal, and the weather and sea had little effect on them. The Greeks had a plentiful supply of <blank data-qid="q25"/> which they mined themselves, but were able to avoid the problem of <blank data-qid="q26"/> which the Spanish encountered much later, after they allowed too much gold from the New World to enter their economy.

question_groups.instruction:
Reading Passage 3 has nine paragraphs, A-I.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Choose the correct letter, A-I, in boxes 27-33.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

items.prompt_html: details of an experiment in taste comparison
examples of medications that could be improved using compounds that remove bitterness
a biological reason why some people don't like bitter-tasting foods
an explanation of how the taste process works
why bitterness is more interesting commercially than other tastes
an example of how compounds that remove bitterness could indirectly benefit health
a reason why people have different taste preferences from animals

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the sentences below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Complete the sentences below.

Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

The grapefruit used to make drinks is chosen because it contains a smaller amount of a substance called <blank data-qid="q34"/>

Animals associate a bitter taste with <blank data-qid="q35"/> plants.

Our varying degree of sensitivity to bitter tastes is thought to be <blank data-qid="q36"/>

People who are extremely aware of bitter tastes are called <blank data-qid="q37"/>

Receptors inside the <blank data-qid="q38"/> on the tongue detect the taste of food and drink.

items.prompt_html: The G protein identified by Robert Margolskee

options.label: A
options.text: is responsible for transmitting bitter tastes.

options.label: B
options.text: causes dangerous reactions in the cells of the tongue.

options.label: C
options.text: has a similar function to sight and smell receptors.

options.label: D
options.text: can be found only in sweetness and umami.

items.prompt_html: AMP is an organic compound which is

options.label: C
options.text: able to reduce some bitter tastes
